name: ClearML
tagline: Open-source MLOps suite that auto-logs your experiments, data, and pipelines
metaDescription: ClearML is an open-source MLOps suite. Add two lines to auto-log experiments, metrics, and artifacts, then manage data, orchestration, and serving.
overview: ClearML is an open-source suite for machine-learning development and production. Its core is the Experiment Manager, which automatically logs your runs, hyper-parameters, metrics, console output, and model artifacts once you add a couple of lines to your training script. Around that sit modules for data management, orchestration and pipelines, and model serving, all backed by a ClearML server you can self-host or use through the free hosted tier.

It is aimed at ML and deep-learning engineers and teams who want experiment tracking without rewriting their code. ClearML hooks into common frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, scikit-learn, XGBoost, and LightGBM, and captures Tensorboard scalars, Matplotlib figures, and resource usage automatically.

In the experiment-tracking category, ClearML stands out by bundling tracking with the rest of an MLOps stack: the same platform that records a run can also version the dataset behind it, queue the job on a remote agent, and serve the resulting model.
features: Automatic experiment logging from two lines of code: source control state, installed packages, hyper-parameters, and initial model weights
Captures stdout/stderr, CPU/GPU resource monitoring, model snapshots, and artifacts, with optional upload to S3, Google Storage, Azure, or a shared folder
Logs Tensorboard/TensorboardX scalars, metrics, histograms, images, audio, and video, plus Matplotlib and Seaborn figures
Hyper-parameter capture for argparse, Click, Python Fire, Hydra, and TensorFlow defines
Built-in integrations for PyTorch (incl. Ignite and Lightning), TensorFlow, Keras, FastAI, XGBoost, LightGBM, and scikit-learn
Wider MLOps modules: data versioning, orchestration and pipelines, model serving, and reports, all on a self-hostable ClearML server

gettingStarted.intro: Install the Python package, connect it to a ClearML server (the free hosted tier or your own), and add a Task to your training script.

steps.heading: Connect to a ClearML server
steps.body: Run the init wizard and paste the credentials from your ClearML workspace (the free hosted app or a self-hosted server) when prompted.

steps.heading: Add tracking to your script
steps.body: Create a Task at the start of your code. From there ClearML auto-logs the run, its environment, hyper-parameters, metrics, and artifacts.
steps.code:
from clearml import Task

task = Task.init(project_name="my project", task_name="my experiment")

useCases: Track and compare deep-learning training runs without changing your existing PyTorch or TensorFlow code
Give a team a shared, searchable history of experiments, metrics, and model snapshots
Version datasets and tie each run back to the exact data it used
Queue and orchestrate training jobs on remote agents across cloud, Kubernetes, or bare-metal machines
